title: spergularia diandra subsp. campestris
intro: Spergularia diandra subsp. campestris is a subspecies of the annual or biennial plant, Spergularia diandra, commonly known as sand spurry or seaside spurry. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Spergularia diandra subsp. campestris include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use as a diuretic to reduce swelling and ease pain. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically made from the leaves and stems of the plant.
